_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Perform analysis of log files from a variety of sources (e.g., individual host logs, network traffic logs, firewall logs, and intrusion detection system [IDS] logs) to identify possible threats to network security.
reference_document_element_description: Implement AI application security controls, including prompt injection defenses, output filtering, access controls, and audit logging.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-T-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-S-012 (TF, cosine: 0.502), TF-A-002 (TF, cosine: 0.493).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.4/10 (raw 0.436). Shared vocabulary: [security]. DCWF-only terms [analysis, detection, files, firewall, host, identify] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, ai, application, audit, controls, defenses] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.869 (P=0.862, R=0.875). Balanced P/R — the 32-word DCWF element and 16-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (86.3%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Perform analysis of log files from a variety of sources (e.g., individual host l" and "Implement AI application security controls, including prompt injection defenses,". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.530 > STS 0.436 (gap +0.094). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [security] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.0%. Shared tokens: [security]. DCWF-only: [analysis, detection, files, firewall, host, identify]. WIDAI-only: [access, ai, application, audit, controls, defenses].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 86.3% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
